evals.prompt: Create a ragbits evaluation project called retrieval-bench that measures precision, recall, and F1 of a document search pipeline over a HuggingFace RAG dataset.
evals.expected_output: A retrieval-bench/ directory with pyproject.toml (ragbits-evaluate + ragbits-core + ragbits-document-search), README.md, retrieval_bench/__init__.py, and retrieval_bench/evaluate.py. evaluate.py builds a factory-style config dict with a DocumentSearchDataLoader (HuggingFaceSource), a DocumentSearchPipeline (vector_store + embedder + ingest_strategy + parser_router + source), and a DocumentSearchPrecisionRecallF1 metric with a RougeChunkMatch strategy. The main block calls Evaluator.run_from_config and prints both results.metrics and results.time_perf. Dependencies installed.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: retrieval-bench/ directory exists

assertions.name: evaluate_file_exists
assertions.description: retrieval_bench/evaluate.py exists and imports Evaluator from ragbits.evaluate.evaluator

assertions.name: init_file_exists
assertions.description: retrieval_bench/__init__.py exists so the directory is a valid package

assertions.name: document_search_deps_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-evaluate, ragbits-core, and ragbits-document-search

assertions.name: factory_config_uses_document_search_types
assertions.description: The config dict references ragbits.evaluate.dataloaders.document_search:DocumentSearchDataLoader, ragbits.evaluate.pipelines.document_search:DocumentSearchPipeline, and ragbits.evaluate.metrics.document_search:DocumentSearchPrecisionRecallF1 via the type: string pattern

assertions.name: matching_strategy_configured
assertions.description: The precision/recall metric's config contains a matching_strategy (RougeChunkMatch or equivalent) with an explicit threshold

assertions.name: huggingface_source_used
assertions.description: Both dataloader and pipeline reference ragbits.core.sources:HuggingFaceSource with a concrete dataset path (deepsense-ai/synthetic-rag-dataset_v1.0 or a user-supplied equivalent)

assertions.name: evaluator_run_from_config_called
assertions.description: evaluate.py's async main awaits Evaluator.run_from_config(config) and prints results.metrics plus results.time_perf fields (total_time_in_seconds, samples_per_second, latency_in_seconds)

assertions.name: no_question_answer_imports
assertions.description: evaluate.py does NOT import question-answer dataloaders, pipelines, or metrics — the target is document-search only

assertions.name: readme_documents_metric_interpretation
assertions.description: README.md tells the user what precision / recall / F1 mean in this context and how to swap the dataloader source for their own test set

evals.prompt: Build a ragbits evaluation called qa-judge that uses LLM-as-judge metrics (correctness, faithfulness, relevance) to score answer quality from our support bot. Use gpt-4o-mini as the judge model.
evals.expected_output: A qa-judge/ directory. pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-evaluate and ragbits-core (document-search optional if the QA pipeline retrieves). evaluate.py configures a QuestionAnswerDataLoader sourced from a HuggingFace dataset, a QA pipeline whose answer LLM is separate from the judge LLM, and three metrics — QuestionAnswerAnswerCorrectness, QuestionAnswerAnswerFaithfulness, and QuestionAnswerAnswerRelevance — each with its own llm_judge config referencing gpt-4o-mini. README surfaces the expected LLM call count and cost caveat.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: qa-judge/ directory exists

assertions.name: evaluate_deps_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-evaluate and ragbits-core

assertions.name: qa_dataloader_used
assertions.description: config.dataloader.type references ragbits.evaluate.dataloaders.question_answer:QuestionAnswerDataLoader (or a subclass)

assertions.name: three_judge_metrics_wired
assertions.description: config.metrics contains three entries referencing QuestionAnswerAnswerCorrectness, QuestionAnswerAnswerFaithfulness, and QuestionAnswerAnswerRelevance from ragbits.evaluate.metrics.question_answer

assertions.name: judge_model_is_gpt_4o_mini
assertions.description: Each metric's llm_judge config references gpt-4o-mini via LiteLLM

assertions.name: judge_distinct_from_answer_model
assertions.description: The pipeline's answer LLM and the metric judge LLMs are declared as separate config entries — the judge is not scoring its own output by construction

assertions.name: readme_documents_cost_caveat
assertions.description: README explicitly mentions that each metric × sample = one LLM call, surfaces the call count calculation, and recommends a cheap judge model

assertions.name: evaluator_run_from_config_called
assertions.description: evaluate.py awaits Evaluator.run_from_config(config) inside an async main and prints results.metrics

evals.prompt: Create a ragbits evaluation called rerank-sweep that measures retrieval F1 on our document corpus and runs an Optuna sweep over the retrieval k (1-10) and score_threshold (-1.5 to -0.5) to find the best config.
evals.expected_output: A rerank-sweep/ directory with pyproject.toml (ragbits-evaluate + ragbits-core + ragbits-document-search), evaluate.py (baseline document-search evaluation with precision/recall/F1), and optimize.py (Optuna sweep). optimize.py uses the same dataloader and metrics as evaluate.py but marks vector_store.default_options.k and score_threshold with {'optimize': True, 'range': [...]}. The main block calls Optimizer.run_from_config and persists results via log_optimization_to_file. README documents n_trials budget and how to promote a winning config back into evaluate.py.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: rerank-sweep/ directory exists

assertions.name: both_scripts_exist
assertions.description: Both rerank_sweep/evaluate.py and rerank_sweep/optimize.py exist

assertions.name: optimizer_import_present
assertions.description: optimize.py imports Optimizer from ragbits.evaluate.optimizer and log_optimization_to_file from ragbits.evaluate.utils

assertions.name: optimizer_direction_maximize
assertions.description: optimize.py's config contains an optimizer block with direction: 'maximize' and an n_trials value

assertions.name: k_is_optimized
assertions.description: optimize.py marks vector_store.default_options.k with {'optimize': True, 'range': [1, 10]} (or an equivalent shape)

assertions.name: score_threshold_is_optimized
assertions.description: optimize.py marks vector_store.default_options.score_threshold with {'optimize': True, 'range': [-1.5, -0.5]}

assertions.name: optimizer_run_from_config_called
assertions.description: optimize.py's main calls Optimizer.run_from_config(config) and writes the result via log_optimization_to_file

assertions.name: evaluate_and_optimize_share_dataloader_and_metrics
assertions.description: The dataloader and metrics blocks in optimize.py match evaluate.py — the sweep measures the same thing the baseline measures

assertions.name: readme_documents_trial_budget
assertions.description: README surfaces n_trials × per-trial cost budgeting guidance and tells the user how to copy a winning config back into evaluate.py
